column1: How identifiers and records flow between PMBOK and DM.

column5: PMBOK.project.intake_id; PMBOK.change_request.intake_id (optional); referenced in every PMBOK row indirectly via project_id
column6: Assigned at triage in DM. Propagates to PMBOK on charter creation. Persists for life of project.
Theragen Doc ID — the human-readable controlled-doc identifier. PMBOK references it when an artifact also lives in DM.

column5: PMBOK.stakeholder.person_id; PMBOK.project.sponsor_person_id, project_manager_id; PMBOK.* (owner / approver columns); PMBOK.approval.approver_person_id
column6: Person directory lives in DM. PMBOK never duplicates person data.

column5: PMBOK.* (department columns use ENUM mirror for query speed)
column6: Department list lives in DM. PMBOK uses an enum mirror for performance; values must stay in sync.
Compliance frame catalog lives in DM. PMBOK references frames as enum values.

column5: PMBOK.change_request — distinct entity (project CR vs governance CR)
column6: Project-level CRs (PMBOK) and document-governance CRs (DM) are separate. If a project CR triggers a doc change, the DM CR references the PMBOK CR via affected_artifacts text field.
